macro_summary: Oil surged 10% to $109.40 after US-Iran diplomacy collapsed and the White House planned an extended blockade of Iranian ports, re-validating the Hormuz supply threat. BigTech earnings delivered a split verdict: GOOG rose on cloud strength, while MSFT and AMZN slumped after MSFT disclosed that OpenAI constitutes 45% of Azure's commercial backlog, deepening capex-ROI concerns. The Fed pushed rate-cut expectations to late 2026 as the oil shock hardens the inflation narrative; the US10Y climbed to 4.42%.
commodities_note: WTI spiked 10.1% to $109.40, the largest single-session move in the tracking period, as the Hormuz blockade extends. Gold fell 0.8% to $4,571.40, and the DXY firmed 0.3% to 98.99 on the rate-haven bid.

writeup.headline: Promoted to AVOID — MSFT disclosed OpenAI is 45% of Azure backlog.
writeup.what_to_do: Promoted to AVOID after MSFT's April 29 earnings call revealed that OpenAI represents 45% of Azure's commercial backlog, confirming the revenue circularity risk and concentration vulnerability. The capex moderation threat is now validated. Avoid until either Azure ex-OpenAI growth accelerates meaningfully or the stock drops to ~$380 (20% below current) to discount the impaired thesis.

writeup.headline: Promoted to AVOID — China suspended autonomous driving permits.
writeup.what_to_do: Promoted to AVOID after Bloomberg reported on April 29 that China suspended all new autonomous driving permits following a Baidu Apollo Go outage. This directly blocks WeRide's fleet expansion thesis and stalls commercial scaling. Avoid until permits are reinstated or the stock drops below $6.85 to price in the regulatory impairment.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks remain under pressure as NIM compression enters day 44. US10Y at 4.42% and DXY 98.99 reinforce the dual headwind. DBS reports today — its NIM and guidance will set the tone for OCBC and UOB next week.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — dual reset conditions (US10Y <4.20% + STI >5000) not met.
singapore.key_development: DBS reports pre-open today; the NIM trajectory is the binary that decides whether the cluster's mean-reversion window opens or closes.

semiconductors.summary: Semis bifurcation intensifies. Mid-cap foundry names (ASML, TSM, MU) hold structurally despite oil logistics cost creep, while mega-cap AI plays (AMD +72% 1mo, INTC +130% 1mo) stretch extension to extreme levels. LITE's earnings and NVDA's photonics catalyst are the next cluster triggers.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Bifurcated — mid-cap foundry structural demand intact; mega-cap duration risk elevated.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD enters earnings May 6 at +47% extension; no pullback has occurred to reset the entry gate.

big_tech.summary: April 30 earnings split the cluster. GOOG rose on TPU v7 momentum and the Meta deal, while MSFT's disclosure that OpenAI is 45% of Azure backlog crystallised revenue-circularity fears and AMZN slumped as AI capex undermined free cash flow. The $1.7T hyperscaler capex thesis faces its first serious credibility test.
big_tech.thesis_status: Under pressure — MSFT promoted to AVOID; the cluster's capex narrative is directly challenged.
big_tech.key_development: MSFT's OpenAI concentration revelation is the most consequential development of earnings week — it turns a known risk into a confirmed structural headwind.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA $2B photonics partner alongside LITE; any AI datacenter optics contract wins benefit both.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 8-10% from current levels or if LITE's May 6 earnings confirm photonics order acceleration.

interconnected.name: SK Hynix
interconnected.reason: Leading HBM supplier to NVDA; HBM4 ramp and premium pricing directly impact MU's competitive position.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after MU's next earnings (June 25) for memory-cycle pricing confirmation; pullback to 200-day moving average would offer a better risk/reward.

interconnected.name: ExxonMobil
interconnected.reason: Direct beneficiary of $109+ oil; the Hormuz disruption structurally elevates integrated oil margins.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if XOM pulls back 5% from its oil-spike peak on any de-escalation headline; use the oil price as the primary signal.

active_risks: Iran/Hormuz blockade drives WTI above $109, disrupting global crude and LNG flows.
Federal Reserve delays rate cuts to late 2026 as oil shock re-ignites inflation concerns.
BigTech earnings split exposes AI capex ROI credibility gap across the hyperscaler complex.
Singapore bank NIM compression persists amid elevated US rates and MAS S$NEER appreciation.
geopolitical.market_impact: Elevated oil is widening the rate premium — the US10Y at 4.42% is punishing capex-heavy mega-caps like AMZN and MSFT while BigTech in aggregate struggles to justify $1.7T in AI spend. Semis cluster is bifurcated, with only mid-cap foundry names holding up. Singapore banks are stuck in a NIM compression loop with no relief in sight, trapping capital.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: MSFT earnings revealed OpenAI represents 45% of Azure’s commercial backlog, triggering its AVOID downgrade. WTI spiked 10.1% — the largest single-day move in tracking — on the US plan to extend the Iran port blockade.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Deploy capital selectively only on names where structural demand (foundry, photonics) meets a technical pullback; avoid energy-overextended positions and any mega-cap with capex ROI uncertainty until FOMC clarity.

events_this_week.event: DBS (D05.SI) reports pre-open — NIM trajectory critical
PLTR earnings — defense AI spending validation
AMD earnings — Meta GPU deal revenue scale
FOMC Rate Decision — last Powell press conference
